stimulus: Inventor Hedy Lamarr created an early prototype of frequency-hopping signaling by inserting a perforated paper roll into a transmitter and, using a synchronized timer, advancing the signal smoothly across the ______blank When the timer and paper roll were activated together, the resulting pattern of shifting frequencies made the transmission far harder to jam.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The convention being tested is the use of plural and possessive nouns. The singular possessive noun "roll’s" and the plural noun "perforations" correctly indicate that there is only one paper roll and it has multiple perforations.

Choice A is incorrect because the context requires the plural noun "perforations," not the singular possessive noun "perforation’s." Choice C is incorrect because the context requires the singular possessive noun "roll’s," not the plural possessive noun "rolls’." Choice D is incorrect because the context requires the singular possessive noun "roll’s" and the plural noun "perforations," not the plural noun "rolls" or the plural possessive noun "perforations’."
